question_text: Let $( X _ { 1 } , X _ { 2 } , X _ { 3 } )$ have $N _ { 3 } ( { \pmb \mu } , { \pmb \Sigma } )$ distribution with $\pmb { \mu } = \begin{array} { l } { \left[ \begin{array} { l } { 2 } \\ { - 3 } \\ { 1 } \end{array} \right] } \end{array}$ and $\Sigma = \left[ { \begin{array} { c c c } { 2 5 } & { - 2 } & { 4 } \\ { - 2 } & { 4 } & { 1 } \\ { 4 } & { 1 } & { 9 } \end{array} } \right]$ . For which of the following vectors ??, $X _ { 2 }$ and $X _ { 2 } - \pmb { a } ^ { T } \left[ { \pmb X } _ { 1 } \right]$ are independent?

options.A: $ \pmb { a } = \left[ \begin{array} { l } { 0 } \\ { 1 } \end{array} \right]\tag{B} $ $ \pmb { a } = \left[ - 1 \atop - 1 \right]\tag{C} $ $ \pmb { a } = \left[ \begin{array} { l } { 0 } \\ { 2 } \end{array} \right]\tag{D} $ $ \pmb { a } = \left[ ^ { 2 } _ { 2 } \right] $

solution:
To determine for which vector \(\pmb{a}\), \(X_2\) and \(X_2 - \pmb{a}^T \left[ \pmod{X_1} \right]\) are independent, we need to check the condition for the covariance between \(X_2\) and \(X_2 - \pmod{X_1}\). If the covariance is zero, then the two variables are independent.

The covariance between \(X_2\) and \(X_2 - \pmod{X_1}\) is given by:
\[
\text{Cov}(X_2, X_2 - \pmod{X_1}) = \text{Cov}(X_2, X_2) - \text{Cov}(X_2, \pmod{X_1})
\]
Since \(\text{Cov}(X_2, X_2) = \text{Var}(X_2)\), we need to find \(\text{Cov}(X_2, \pmod{X_1})\). The conditional expectation \(\pmod{X_1}\) is a linear function of \(X_1\), so we can write:
\[
\pmod{X_1} = \pmod{X_2 \mid X_1} = \mu_2 + \pmod{\pmod{X_2} - \mu_2 \mid X_1}
\]
The conditional expectation \(\pmod{\pmod{X_2} - \mu_2 \mid X_1}\) is a linear combination of \(X_1\), and we can use the formula for the conditional expectation of a multivariate normal distribution:
\[
\pmod{X_2 \mid X_1} = \mu_2 + \pmod{\pmod{X_2} - \mu_2 \mid X_1} = \mu_2 + \Sigma_{21} \Sigma_{11}^{-1} (X_1 - \mu_1)
\]
where \(\Sigma_{21}\) is the covariance vector between \(X_2\) and \(X_1\), and \(\Sigma_{11}\) is the variance of \(X_1\). From the given covariance matrix \(\Sigma\), we have:
\[
\mu_1 = \begin{pmatrix} 2 \\ -3 \\ 1 \end{pmatrix}, \quad \mu_2 = -3, \quad \Sigma_{21} = \begin{pmatrix} -2 \\ 4 \\ 1 \end{pmatrix}, \quad \Sigma_{11} = 25
\]
Thus,
\[
\pmod{X_2 \mid X_1} = -3 + \frac{1}{25} \begin{pmatrix} -2 \\ 4 \\ 1 \end{pmatrix}^T (X_1 - \begin{pmatrix} 2 \\ -3 \\ 1 \end{pmatrix}) = -3 + \frac{1}{25} (-2X_1 + 4X_2 + X_3 - 4 + 9 - 1) = -
